column6: description

— Ported ArrayUtils from flexmark-java: contained, append, firstOf, indexOf, lastOf, lastIndexOf, toArray — all methods faithfully translated with Scala idioms (ClassTag for array creation, Nullable for nullable returns)

CommonMark spec.txt and spec.0.26..0.30.txt are present as test resources but not loaded by any runner — ssg-md has zero CommonMark conformance tests. Only flexmark's internal ast_spec.md is run. Headline gap from docs/architecture/flexmark-port.md Gap Analysis (2026-04-07).

— Fully ported from flexmark AttributesNodeFormatter.java — all 675 lines including PhasedNodeFormatter, ExplicitAttributeIdProvider, translation span mapping, attribute uniquification, format options (sort/spacing/quotes/id/class format), consecutive attribute combination

— Fully ported from flexmark SimTocNodeFormatter.java — handles REMOVE, UPDATE, AS_IS modes with proper TocOptions creation via fromOptions, SimTocGenerateOnFormat enum, and renderTocHeaders delegation
— normalizeEOL/normalizeEndWithEOL ported from Escaping.java — CR/CRLF→LF conversion with ReplacedTextMapper tracking. Removed stale 'not yet ported' comments since Html5Entities already existed.
Nested block-quote prefix handling simplified (lines 79-84). Round-trip formatting of nested > quotes loses structure beyond one level.

withMaxRenderTimeMillis is a no-op: renderToObject never wraps in Future/ExecutorService nor checks the limit. Original Template.java enforces it via Future.get(maxRenderTimeMillis). See docs/architecture/liquid-port-gap-analysis.md §4.3.

include_relative broken from disk: Template never tracks sourceLocation nor populates REGISTRY_ROOT_FOLDER. Original Template.java setRootFolderRegistry does this. IncludeRelative cannot resolve. See gap-analysis §4.3.

Missing render API surface: render(jsonString), render(Inspectable), render(key,value,kvs*), errors() accessor, withContextHolder, toStringTree, getParseTree, render(Map,ExecutorService,bool). Port has only render()/render(JMap)/renderToObject(JMap)/renderToObjectUnguarded. See gap-analysis §4.3.
— EAGER/LAZY now differ: EAGER makes defensive copy (placeholder for LiquidSupport.objectToMap, ISS-015), LAZY passes variables through as-is matching Java's direct-return behavior
— Template now tracks templateSize (set from input.length during parse). renderToObject checks against limitMaxTemplateSizeBytes and throws RuntimeException if exceeded, matching Java original.
— Added missing withLiquidStyleWhere(Boolean) setter to TemplateParser.Builder — follows same pattern as withLiquidStyleInclude

Builder missing setters present in liqp: withDefaultTimeZone, withEnvironmentMapConfigurator, withSnippetsFolderName, withDateParser, withObjectMapper, withStripSingleLine. Port also lacks defaultTimeZone/environmentMapConfigurator/dateParser fields entirely. See gap-analysis §4.1.
— Added parse(Path), parse(File), parse(InputStream), parse(Reader) overloads to TemplateParser — all delegate to parse(String) after reading content

Self-declared stub (line 14: 'initial stub. Full implementation in Phase 4'). TemplateParser.evaluate silently returns an empty HashMap for any non-LiquidSupport input. Combined with Template.renderToObject not calling evaluate at all, Inspectable POJOs are silently dropped. See gap-analysis §4.18.

Inline 70-line rewrite missing major Jekyll/Liquid Where semantics: no dotted property paths (where: 'address.city' looks up literal key), no POJO support (only JMap+Inspectable), no nil/empty/blank literal handling, no collection-property matching, no numeric coercion of string properties. Original is 5 files (Where.java + filters/where/{Jekyll,Liquid,WhereImpl,PropertyResolverHelper,PropertyResolverAdapter}). See gap-analysis §4.11.

Only parses ISO-8601 inputs. Original ships 63 fallback patterns via filters/date/Parser.java + Ruby ordinal normalization + BasicDateParser.getZonedDateTimeFromTemporalAccessor. Inputs like 'March 1st 2024', '01/15/2024', RFC 2822, '15 Jan' no longer parse. The entire filters/date/* package is skipped. See gap-analysis §4.10.
— Added ~20 missing strftime directives: %C %D %F %G %g %V %v %N %R %r %T %c %x %X %+ %s %U %W, plus %:z/%::z/%:::z timezone variants and %0/%_ modifier flags
— Json.toJson now handles NaN/Infinity as null (valid JSON), and TemporalAccessor as quoted string. Remaining gaps (cycle detection, Inspectable) tracked separately.

— Fixed date handling: asRubyDate/asTemporal/temporalAsArray/temporalToString now handle LocalDate, LocalDateTime, OffsetDateTime, Instant, LocalTime via new toZonedDateTime helper ported from liqp BasicDateParser. No more silent fallback to now().
— asFormattedNumber now strips trailing zeros from BigDecimal fractional part (5.50→5.5) matching Java's stripTrailingZeros behavior. Uses string-level stripping for cross-platform safety.
— Include tag Jekyll branch now throws LiquidException for non-Map parameters instead of silently dropping them, matching original Java's ClassCastException behavior with a descriptive message
Missing fields/methods present in liqp: 'template' Template ref, getDateParser() (no BasicDateParser exists in port), getRootFolder() returns Any instead of Path. See gap-analysis §4.4.
— Parser now throws LiquidException with token type, value, and line number instead of silently skipping unexpected tokens

No rule-by-rule grammar coverage verification. Original LiquidParser.g4 has ~46 productions; not exhaustively mapped to Scala parser methods. At-risk rules: id-as-keyword (allows {% assign if = 1 %}), evaluateInOutputTag predicate, output-rule strict/warn/lax alternatives, error_other_tag 4 recovery branches, not_out_end / other_than_tag_end token sets.

Entire spi/* package skipped (TypesSupport, BasicTypesSupport, Java7DateTypesSupport, Java8DateTypesSupport, SPIHelper). No replacement extension mechanism for custom user types in the port. ServiceLoader doesn't work on JS/Native; design a Scala-3 typeclass replacement (given LiquidTypeSupport[T]) or formally drop SPI from scope and document. See gap-analysis §4.16.
filters/date/* skipped (5 files: BasicDateParser, Parser, FuzzyDateDateParser, CustomDateFormatRegistry, CustomDateFormatSupport). Required to fix Date filter parsing — see related Date.scala bugs.

filters/where/* skipped (5 files: WhereImpl, JekyllWhereImpl, LiquidWhereImpl, PropertyResolverAdapter, PropertyResolverHelper). Required to fix Where filter Jekyll/Liquid mode parity — see related Where.scala bug.

Test coverage ~20-30% of liqp. Port has 280 tests in 12 suites; original has 100+ test files (~1500+ assertions). Missing suites: TemplateSuite (covering render overloads, errors(), max-render-time, max-template-size, ContextHolder), DateFilterSuite, WhereFilterSuite, LValueSuite, LexerSuite, ParserSuite, NodeSuite (14 node test files in original), IncludeRelativeSuite, ProtectionSettings full coverage. See gap-analysis §6.
— Documented LIQP→JEKYLL default flavor change in migration notes header comment. SSG targets Jekyll-compatible sites; users needing LIQP semantics should set .withFlavor(Flavor.LIQP).

Multi-pass compression loop unimplemented (TODO at Compressor.scala:1021-1033). Single-pass only; TerserSuite compression tests disabled because the loop hangs. Effectively makes TerserJsCompressor a parse+re-emit, not a minifier.

SymbolDef/scope integration missing across compress passes (~50 TODOs in DropUnused, ReduceVars, Inference, Inline, TightenBody, Evaluate, GlobalDefs). Blocks safe inlining, DCE, constant folding, pure-call elision.
sourcemap.js (149 LOC) not ported. OutputStream lacks position tracking. Source map output impossible.
minify.js orchestration stubbed (93 LOC vs 413). Missing ecma normalization, mangle cache, format options, error shape, full pipeline sequencing.
Specialized compression passes missing: collapse-vars, negate-iife, wrap-iife, hoist_vars, drop_console, logical-assignment lowering.
Terser test/compress/*.js (168 fixtures, thousands of cases) not ingested. ssg-js has only 6 munit suites with ~no compression coverage. Fixtures use a simple DSL trivial to parse.
mozilla-ast.js (2099 LOC) SpiderMonkey AST format not ported. Public API surface gap; low priority.
— preserveLineBreaks now honored: collapseMultiSpaces and removeIntertagSpaces check the option — when true, newlines between tags are preserved as single newlines instead of being removed
— Quote-stripping regex changed from [A-Za-z0-9_-]+ to [^\s=<>"']+ matching htmlcompressor behavior — strips quotes from any value without whitespace/=/angle-brackets/quotes
— Added preserveSsi Boolean + SsiPattern regex to HtmlMinifyOptions for SSI directive preservation. effectivePreservePatterns auto-injects patterns from boolean flags.
CSS color folding (rgb()/rgba()->hex, named colors->hex), shorthand collapsing (margin/padding/font-weight), vendor-prefix culling not implemented.
— Minifier.minify now checks compressCss/compressJs/compressJson toggles — returns input unchanged when toggle is off. Added minifyFile method that also checks exclude patterns against file paths.
— Added preservePhp Boolean + PhpPattern regex to HtmlMinifyOptions. When true, <?php ... ?> blocks are auto-preserved during minification via effectivePreservePatterns.
compress_js_templates option missing. htmlcompressor option to also minify JavaScript template literals (backtick strings) inside <script> blocks. Port has no equivalent flag.

— Fixed insertBefore fallback: all 3 appendChild calls now use textBase.fold(node.insertBefore(newNode))(_.appendChild(newNode)) matching Java's null-check pattern — nodes are no longer silently dropped when textBase is empty

Enhanced CSS mode (cssminify2 v2.1.0+) entirely absent: css_enhanced_mode, css_merge_duplicate_selectors, css_optimize_shorthand_properties, css_advanced_color_optimization, css_preserve_ie_hacks, css_compress_variables. Port's CssMinifyOptions has none of these. Tracked separately from ISS-041 (which covers core gem feature gaps).

Replaces org.nibor.autolink (LinkExtractor) with hand-rolled regex (URL_PATTERN/EMAIL_PATTERN/adjustUrlEnd). Documented for cross-platform but introduces detection gaps vs nibor heuristics: trailing-punctuation rules, IDN/unicode TLDs, scheme allowlist, and surrounding-bracket trimming may diverge. Run flexmark-ext-autolink AstTest fixtures to quantify.

— Added JsCompressor.compress(input, options) overload with JsCompressorOptions trait. MinifyOptions gains jsCompressorOpts field. Minifier.minify passes options through when present. Concrete implementations (e.g. TerserOptions) can extend JsCompressorOptions.
— CSS removeComments now preserves /*! ... */ bang/important comments (license headers) while still removing regular /* ... */ block comments
— JsMinifier now preserves important comments: /*\! ... */ (bang comments) and /* @license ... */ are kept when removeComments=true, matching Terser's default behavior
— Protocol-removal regexes now match cite, longdesc, manifest, formaction, srcset in addition to href/src/action

— collapseMultiSpaces now uses \s{2,} (any whitespace) instead of ' {2,}' (spaces only) when preserveLineBreaks is false — matches htmlcompressor's remove_multi_spaces behavior that collapses all whitespace runs

Preserved-tag list is fixed at pre/textarea/script/style. htmlcompressor preserves additional content-sensitive tags depending on options (e.g. svg/math content in some configurations). Port has no configurable preserved-tag set; users must inject regexes via preservePatterns.

Cross-platform regex rewrite slightly more permissive than original Java pattern: '[a-z\d-]{0,37}[a-z\d]' allows consecutive dashes whereas '-(?=[a-z\d])' did not. Acceptable per documented note (matches actual GitHub rules), but flag for test verification.
— Ported getTocPrefix, getSimTocPrefix, markdownHeaderTexts, renderTocContent, renderMarkdownToc from flexmark TocUtils.java — all 5 missing markdown-side helpers now complete
— Added equals/hashCode, withRawTitleLevel, withRawTitle, withLevelList to TocOptions — matching Java original's full API surface

TreeTransformer is identical to TreeWalker — node.transform() method missing entirely. The class adds 'before/after' callback fields but neither does anything different from TreeWalker. Original transform.js (323 LOC, ~50 def_transform clauses) defines per-node-type transform() methods that walk children, then call the after callback to allow node replacement. Without this, any pass that depends on returning a replacement node from a TreeTransformer callback (e.g., GlobalDefs) silently does nothing. Port: AstNode.scala:203-206 vs original transform.js.

GlobalDefs.resolveDefs is dead code: (1) never called from Compressor.compress (no reference to GlobalDefs/resolveDefs anywhere in Compressor.scala) and (2) even if invoked, uses ast.walk(transformer) instead of ast.transform(transformer). The before-callback returns a replacement node, but TreeWalker discards return values used as substitution — only true/WalkAbort affect traversal. Result: global_defs compressor option is silently broken. Two fixes needed: wire resolveDefs into Compressor.compress, and either implement node.transform() or rewrite resolveDefs to mutate parent fields directly. See ISS-057. GlobalDefs.scala:216-260.
— getSymbolName now checks sym.thedef for SymbolDef with non-null mangledName, returning mangledName when available — matches original output.js print_name behavior (def.mangled_name || def.name)

Compressor.optimizeTree (single-pass walker, lines 200-340) only recurses into a small subset of node types: AstScope, AstBlock, AstSimpleStatement, AstIf, AstReturn, AstAssign, AstBinary, AstUnary, AstCall, AstConditional, AstSequence. Missing recursion into: AstFor, AstForIn, AstForOf, AstWhile, AstDo, AstSwitch, AstTry/Catch/Finally, AstClass body, AstFunction/Defun/Arrow body, AstObject/Array literal contents, AstThrow, AstWith, AstLabeledStatement, AstVarDef.value, AstExport.exportedValue, etc. As a result optimizations never fire on expressions inside loops, switches, classes, or function bodies. Compounds with ISS-031: even when single-pass works, coverage is partial. See Compressor.scala:200-340.

Compressor.dropConsole (line 1034-1037) is a literal no-op stub that returns toplevel unchanged. The dropConsole option is parsed and propagated through CompressorOptions but never actually drops console.* calls. Original index.js drop_console removes AST_Call nodes whose expression is a property access on a 'console' SymbolRef. Comment says 'TODO: implement console dropping via TreeTransformer' — also blocked by ISS-057 if reimplemented via TreeTransformer.

— isParagraphWrappingDisabled parameter type changed from Any to ListOptions across the entire hierarchy: ParagraphItemContainer trait, ListItem, TaskListItem, FootnoteBlock, DefinitionTerm, EnumeratedReferenceBlock. ListItem's pattern match removed in favor of direct listOptions.isInTightListItem(node) call.

— ListItem.openingMarker converted from var to protected _openingMarker + def getter/setter. TaskListItem overrides setter to throw IllegalStateException, matching Java's setOpeningMarker override. Constructor uses backing field directly.
— Replaced raw null with new MutableDataSet() in no-arg constructor — semantically equivalent (all DataKey.get calls return defaults) but avoids the no-null rule violation
— Fixed break-as-comment bug: wrapped while loop in boundary{} and replaced no-op 'lastFound // break' with break() for proper early exit on non-contiguous matches

— All 3 gaps fixed: (1) super-ctor now passes ATTRIBUTE_UNIQUIFICATION_CATEGORY_MAP, (2) renderReferenceText uses AttributesNodeFormatter.getEncodedIdAttribute for proper encoded id transformation, (3) Factory.afterDependents declares AttributesNodeFormatter.Factory dependency
— Fixed in ISS-066 resolution: renderReferenceBlock now uses markdown.appendNonTranslating(node.text) instead of append(node.text)

— Fixed behavioral bug: havePreviousOpener/haveNextCloser now return canOpen/canClose result on FIRST matching delimiter (matching Java behavior) instead of continuing search on false. Replaced return with boundary/break.
— Fixed as part of ISS-062: EnumeratedReferenceBlock.isParagraphWrappingDisabled uses ListOptions
— AsideBlock(chars, segments) constructor now sets contentLines = segments, matching Java's super(chars, segments) call

Java uses java.net.URL.getFile() (which returns rawPath+? +rawQuery if present, '' otherwise). Scala port emulates with URI getRawPath + (if query then '?'+query else ''). For typical youtu.be URLs results match. Edge case: URI parsing accepts more inputs than URL — some malformed strings that Java would catch as MalformedURLException now silently succeed via URI. Consider adding explicit scheme/host validation.
— MacroDefinitionBlock(chars, segments) constructor now sets contentLines = segments, matching Java's super(chars, segments) call

Placeholder ExtensionSuite only — tests construction and a single getType call. No spec-driven rendering tests for ordinal generation, cross-document references, compound types (a:b:c), or HTML output (anchor href, title via EnumRefTextCollectingVisitor). Original flexmark has Combo/Spec test suites with hundreds of fixtures; SSG has none. The renderer and formatter gaps would not surface without rendering tests.

Multiple uses of null.asInstanceOf[MacroDefinitionBlock] and Nullable.empty.getOrElse(null) to satisfy ReferencingNode trait return type. Trait should be re-typed to return Nullable[B] (out of group C scope but tracked here).
Placeholder ExtensionSuite only — tests prefixes and Utilities mime lookup. No end-to-end Markdown→HTML render tests for \![alt](url1|url2)A producing ,

Parser omits GitLab block-quote pass-through logic in tryContinue (Java lines 58-65). When close marker '<<<' is reached but the last child is an open GitLabBlockQuote, original lets the child handle the line; SSG always closes. Marked 'not yet ported' but should be tracked once GitLab ext is added.

Placeholder ExtensionSuite — tests node construction only. No tests for IMAGE_PATTERN matching ![alt](url =100x200) syntax or rendering to . Combo/Spec tests not ported. Cross-platform regex behavior (re2 vs Java) for the IMAGE_PATTERN is unverified.

Placeholder ExtensionSuite — only construction tests. No tests for @[text](https://youtu.be/ID) or @[text](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=ID) post-processing or iframe rendering. The URL→URI substitution in the renderer (port-specific) is unverified, and the youtu.be vs youtube.com/watch branching has no fixtures.
— Fixed as part of ISS-062: ParagraphItemContainer now uses ListOptions, stale comment removed

astExtra prints typographic info only when defined (foreach), but Java original always prints ' typographic: <value> ' even when typographicText is null (printing 'null'). May affect AST snapshot tests.
— Fixed inverted comparison: addFirstReferenceOffset now uses > instead of <, keeping the smallest offset as intended. Bug exists in original Java too; fixed per CLAUDE.md fix-bugs policy.
astExtra getOrElse('') prints empty when typographicOpening/Closing is None; Java prints 'null'. Snapshot AST tests may diverge.
— Fixed translation semantics: node.info uses appendNonTranslating, node.title uses appendTranslating — matching original Java AdmonitionNodeFormatter

— Fixed break-as-comment bug in both link-resolver and content-resolver loops: wrapped each with boundary{}/break() so iteration stops on first non-UNKNOWN status, matching original Java break behavior
— Removed unnecessary child.isDefined guard on else branch — hasChildren guard ensures firstChild is always defined, matching Java's unconditional else
— Added DependencyResolver.resolveFlatDependencies for both LINK_RESOLVER_FACTORIES and CONTENT_RESOLVER_FACTORIES
— Replaced null with new MutableDataSet() in MacroDefinitionRepository default DataKey constructor, avoiding raw null at non-interop boundary
— Fixed blank-line handling: now checks isEmpty || firstChild.exists(_.next.isEmpty) matching Java's isEmpty || firstChild.getNext()==null condition
— Fixed missing break: propagate-loose-tight loop now uses boundary/break to exit on !blankLinesInAST, matching Java original
— Fixed as part of ISS-062: ParagraphItemContainer.isParagraphWrappingDisabled uses ListOptions instead of Any

alt-attribute string built as 'emoji ' + category + ':' + shortcut. Java passes possibly-null category/shortcut yielding literal 'null' substrings. Scala uses getOrElse("") yielding empty substrings. CAUTION comment in source notes string is consumed by Apple Mail HTML parser — different bytes may break round-trip.
— Documented Emoji→EmojiData inner class rename in migration notes. Breaking change needed to avoid name clash with outer Emoji node class.

— Fixed delegate resolution: DelegatingNodeRendererFactoryWrapper.nodeRenderers changed to private[html], HtmlRenderer now creates wrappers with Nullable.empty then sets the complete list on all wrappers before DependencyResolver.resolveFlatDependencies — beforeDependents now correctly resolves delegate ordering

— Fixed early-exit bug: wrapped preProcessParagraph body in boundary{} and replaced 'continue = false // return' with break(()) at both unlink points — inner for loop now exits immediately when block is removed, matching Java's return behavior
— getReferenceNode(Document) now delegates to getReferenceNode(Parser.REFERENCES.get(document)) as in the Java original — Parser.REFERENCES was already ported
— anchorRefText now reads HEADER_ID_REF_TEXT_TRIM_LEADING_SPACES/TRAILING_SPACES from document options instead of hardcoding true

— Fixed collectedNodes population: changed from val to var, added collectNodeTypes HashSet to accumulate nodeClasses during formatter loop, and added post-loop NodeCollectingVisitor.collect() + getSubClassingBag() to populate collectedNodes — nodesOfType()/reversedNodesOfType() now work correctly
Drops IDN.toASCII punicode handling and trailing-slash preservation from Java convertUnicodeURLToAscii; also bypasses Relative_Url inheritance. Unicode domain URLs will fail.
— Relative_Url ported: URI normalization, query/anchor split, toASCIIString, Inspectable support, addError/STRICT mode. Absolute_Url now extends Relative_Url with convertUnicodeURLToAscii.
— Split filter now strips leading empty string when input begins with delimiter, matching Java's lookbehind behavior without re2-incompatible regex.
— matchCondition now uses singletonMap (no parent var copying) + renderToObjectUnguarded with parent context + strict 'true'.equals check — matching Java original's exact semantics
— IncludeRelative.detectSource now resolves paths relative to context.getRootFolder(), falling back to cwd. Include.scala refactored with detectSource hook.

optimizeBinary missing ~15 optimization paths: 32-bit equality, bitwise De Morgan, associative folding, template concat, boolean-context +, comparisons negation, unsafe_comps reversal, right-assoc flattening, a+-b to a-b

optimizeCall missing ~13 optimization paths: unused arg trimming, console.assert folding, Array.from/Array(n), String()/Number() 0-arg, Symbol trimming, RegExp ctor, method call opts (.toString/.join/.charAt/.apply/.call), unsafe_Function, inline_array_like_spread on args

optimizeConditional missing ~12 optimization paths: condition negation swap, x?x:y to x||y, common-assign merge, single-arg-diff call merge, nested ternary flattening, nullish coalescing a==null?b:a to a??b, sequence/binary dedup patterns
optimizeIf missing 4 paths: negated condition preference (negated_is_best), empty-body-alt to || expr, aborts(body) hoist-alt, aborts(alternative) swap-hoist. Port covers ~67pct of original branches
optimizeUnaryPrefix missing 3 paths: negate binary in boolean context (best_of with negate), tilde-xor distributivity (~(x^y) => x^~y), is_32_bit_integer check for double-tilde

maintain_this_binding never called in port: original uses it in AST_Conditional, AST_Binary (&&/||), AST_Sequence to preserve this-context during expression rewriting. May produce incorrect code for method references
optimizeSub missing arguments[n] to named-param optimization and array-literal index flattening ([a,b,c][1] => side-effects + b)
optimizeDot missing unsafe_proto optimization (Array.prototype.foo => [].foo etc) and PropAccess-within-PropAccess early return guard
optimizeAssign missing dead_code elimination (assignment to unreachable var in exit context) and lift_sequences call
optimizeObjectKeyVal missing unsafe_methods: p:function(){} to p(){} and p:()=>{} to p(){} concise method conversion
optimizeChain incomplete: original replaces entirely-nullish chain with void 0 (or 0 for delete context). Port only unwraps optional flag when receiver is non-nullish

optimizeDo truthy-condition path: original wraps body+condition in for-block to preserve condition side effects as trailing statement. Port creates bare AstFor without condition side-effect preservation
CRITICAL: scope.walk(tt) instead of scope.transform(tt) at line ~336. Transformer output discarded, making drop-unused a no-op. Original: self.transform(tt) at drop-unused.js:464.
resolveFixedValue uses Java reflection (getClass.getMethod/invoke) at lines 292-310. Breaks on JS/Native, calls wrong receiver (thedef not SymbolRef). Replace with AstSymbolRef.fixedValue().
find_variable, is_atomic, unsafe_undefined_ref helpers missing. optimizeInfinity/NaN/Undefined replace globals even when locally shadowed (var Infinity=5). Port from index.js:655-665,2963-2982.
optimizeBlockStatement has inverted hasDirective("use strict") check at line ~760. \!hasDirective(...).isInstanceOf[AstNode] is backwards. Should be hasDirective("use strict") == null.

collapse_vars pass (~693 LOC) entirely missing. tighten-body.js:278-970 contains collapse() with 19 sub-functions (scanner, multi_replacer, extract_candidates, find_stop, etc). Most impactful single optimization in the compressor.

negate() entirely missing (~80 LOC). inference.js:836-917. Dispatches per-type: UnaryPrefix unwrap, Sequence negate-last, Conditional negate-both, Binary flip-operator/DeMorgan. Used by optimizeIf, optimizeConditional, optimizeUnaryPrefix.
bitwise_negate() entirely missing (~35 LOC). inference.js:919-954. Folds ~~x, constant-folds ~N. Used by optimizeBinary De Morgan paths.

is_callee_pure + global_pure_fns entirely missing (~30 LOC). inference.js:956-987. Blocks dead-call elimination — has_side_effects(AstCall) and may_throw(AstCall) are stubbed always-true because this is absent.
is_call_pure entirely missing (~20 LOC). inference.js:990-1010. Determines if a method call on a native object (Array/String/Number/etc) is pure. Needed by has_side_effects and drop-side-effect-free.

is_constant_expression + all_refs_local entirely missing (~75 LOC). inference.js:671-745. Walks AST checking for out-of-scope references. Used by reduce_vars for inlining decisions and by inline.js. Evaluate.scala has a misplaced incomplete stub.

contains_this entirely missing (~12 LOC). inference.js:1045-1056. Walks tree for AST_This, stops at non-Arrow scopes. Required by is_modified and inline.js. NOTE: Compressor.scala has a private containsThis() but Inference.scala (the canonical location) does not.

handle_defined_after_hoist (~140 LOC) entirely missing. reduce-vars.js:518-660. Two-pass analysis detecting when hoisted fn uses var written after call site. Never called from markLambda or Toplevel handler. Without it, reduce_vars may incorrectly inline variables.

inlineIntoCall missing body-flattening path (~200 LOC). inline.js:490-674 — can_flatten_body, can_inject_args, can_inject_vars, can_inject_symbols, append_var, flatten_args, flatten_vars, flatten_fn. Also missing: reduce_vars symbol resolution, dont_inline_lambda_in_loop guard, negate_iife.

inlineIntoSymbolRef missing 15+ paths. inline.js:168-320 — missing: in_computed_key guard, is_const_symbol_short_than_init_value, is_callee_pure/_NOINLINE on single_use, cross-scope class return, retain_top_func/escaped/INLINED block, DefClass→ClassExpression, Defun→Function, recursive-ref rewriting, clone-to-scope, AST_This multi-use, evaluate+unsafe_regexp, size-based overhead calc.

hoist_declarations (~134 LOC) and hoist_properties (~67 LOC) entirely missing. index.js:739-941. before() has comment "deferred ISS-035". Hoists var decls to function top, merges obj.a/obj.b to individual vars.

bestOf(compressor,ast1,ast2) missing. common.js:186-192. Dispatches to bestOfStatement or bestOfExpression based on firstInStatement. Also: isRefOf always returns false (TODO stub at line 420), isReachable uses wrong algorithm (walk instead of walk_parent, async/generator guard dropped).

has_side_effects(AstCall) always returns true (stub at line ~343). may_throw(AstCall) always returns true (stub at line ~511). Both need is_callee_pure (ISS-122). Also missing: AstAssign use-strict+SymbolRef guard in may_throw, aborts() missing AstSwitchBranch/AstDefClass/AstClassStaticBlock/AstIf cases, _dot_throw missing AstObject/AstExpansion/AstDot cases.

evalSymbolRef is complete stub (always returns self, line ~468). No fixed_value lookup, no escaped depth check, reentrantRefEval set declared but never mutated. Also missing: AST_Function unsafe fn-object creation, RegExp/global_objs eval paths, BigInt guard in evalBinary, NaN+AST_With guard.

dropCall is_callee_pure/is_call_pure stubbed (TODO at line ~259). dropClass missing is_self_referential check. dropAssign missing use-strict+PropAccess guard. dropUnary missing IIFE-in-statement negate branch. AstClassPrivateProperty missing computed key branch.

Major gaps beyond the walk/transform bug: drop_funcs/drop_vars as distinct flags missing, assign_as_unused never called, uses_arguments→argname mark missing, in_export parent check missing, var_defs_by_id tracking missing, AST_Definitions processing in pass 1 missing, lambda argname trimming missing, scan_ref_scoped missing.

Beyond collapse_vars: handleIfReturn has 7/8 patterns missing (can_merge_flow, next_index, prev_index, has_multiple_if_returns, extract_defuns all absent). sequencesize_2 entirely missing. joinConsecutiveVars only handles var+var (8 other join patterns missing). eliminateDeadCode loopcontrol_target stubbed.

figure_out_scope call is TODO stub at line ~219. deepClone is a stub ("for now return node itself"). AstChain case in findDefs drops suffix (bug). SymbolRef global check uses fragile reflection on undeclared field instead of global().

markEscaped missing AstYield escape condition and AstUsingDef in VarDefLike check. reduceSymbolRef \!d.fixed check uses == false instead of falsy (misses null/undefined). reset_def sets singleUse=false instead of undefined/null sentinel.

Missing options: ie8, pure_new, warnings. sequences_limit computation incomplete (get("sequences") returns only boolean, never int count; special case 1→800 absent). CompressorLike missing: in_computed_key(), mangle_options(), sequences_limit field.

optimizeBinary dropped branches: precedence guard on lhs_constants swap, is_strict_comparison flag gating void→null, mask-bits De Morgan check, template+template concat, unsafe_math gate on * associativity, bitwise De Morgan (z&(X|y)), two-level constant fold, x||false&&y conditional, parent-is-&&-with-left check.

selectorPseudoIsSuperselector :is/:matches/:where path uses approximate complexIsSuperselectorComponents instead of full ExtendFunctions.complexIsSuperselector: Dart functions.dart:988-994 calls the real complexIsSuperselector(complex1.components, [...?parents, ComplexSelectorComponent(compound2, const [], compound2.span)]). Scala CompoundSelector.scala:224-231 calls a private complexIsSuperselectorComponents (lines 324-350) that is a simplified greedy-match algorithm ignoring combinators entirely. CloneCssVisitor is a complete stub: every visit method returns the input node unchanged. Dart original (109 LOC) creates deep ModifiableCss copies with new selector mappings from ExtensionStore.clone(). Missing: _visitChildren helper, cloneCssStylesheet() top-level function.

EveryCssVisitor defaults all methods to true but Dart defaults leaf methods (Comment/Declaration/Import) to false and parent methods recurse via node.children.every(). Scala inverts semantics AND drops all child recursion. Every-CSS predicate checks will always return true regardless of content.

rgb()/rgba() 2-arg path missing Dart _rgbTwoArg logic (lines 1388-1423): no isSpecialVariable checks, no isLegacy check/throw for non-legacy colors, no assertLegacy call, no toSpace(rgb) conversion, no isSpecialNumber handling to construct 4-arg function string. Port lines 533-541 just does scalar extraction and changeAlpha.

ExpressionToCalcVisitor is a complete stub (all identity returns). Dart original (65 LOC) wraps modulo in max(math), converts unary +/- for calc, passes InterpolatedFunction/If unchanged. Also missing: top-level expressionToCalc() function wrapping result in FunctionExpression('calc'). Used by error messages and calculation simplifier.

IsCalculationSafeVisitor is a complete stub (all return false). Dart original (90 LOC) returns true for: FunctionExpression, IfExpression, InterpolatedFunctionExpression, LegacyIfExpression, NumberExpression, VariableExpression; validates BinaryOperationExpression for +/-/*/div with recursive left/right check; validates ListExpression (space-sep, no brackets, >1 items); validates StringExpression (not !important, not #id, not U+range, not url()). Critical for calculation evaluation - without it, calc() arguments are never recognized as safe.

rgb()/rgba() 3-4 arg paths use custom scalar() helper instead of Dart _colorFromChannels (line 1370-1384). Dart routes through _colorFromChannels which uses _channelFromValue for unit validation and clamping. Port uses scalar() which only handles % and unitless, missing _channelFromValue behaviors: requiresPercent check, lowerClamped/upperClamped split clamping, proper percentageOrUnitless error messages. Also missing fromRgbFunction=true flag for ColorFormat tracking.
SassParser uses indented-to-SCSS preprocessing instead of faithful port. Original has 19 methods; port replaces ALL with UnsupportedOperationException stubs.

CssParser uses post-parse AST-walk instead of dart-sass inline parse-time rejection. Missing: _disallowedFunctionNames set, _cssFunctionRule, _cssImportRule, parentheses override, identifierLike override.
— Stale: verified fix already in ColorFunctions.scala

IsPlainCssVisitor is a complete stub (all return false). Also missing: _visitArgumentList helper. Used by Expression.isPlainCss which gates Sass-vs-CSS classification.

RecursiveAstVisitor is a complete stub: all 50+ methods are no-ops (return Unit). Dart original (377 LOC) has full recursive traversal: visitAtRootRule visits query+super, visitAtRule visits name+value interpolations+super, visitContentRule visits ArgumentList, visitDebugRule/ErrorRule/WarnRule visit expression, visitDeclaration visits name interpolation+value expression+super, visitEachRule visits list+super, visitForRule visits from+to+super, visitIfRule visits clause expressions+children, visitImportRule visits StaticImport url/modifiers interpolations, visitIncludeRule visits ArgumentList+super, all 18 expression visitors recurse into sub-expressions, all 6 IfCondition visitors recurse, all 11 InterpolatedSelector visitors recurse, plus visitCallableDeclaration, visitArgumentList, visitSupportsCondition, visitInterpolation, visitQualifiedName protected helpers. Without this, any visitor extending RecursiveAstVisitor (like FindDependenciesVisitor's conceptual base) will silently skip expression traversal.

SSG simply falls back to overloads.last when no exact match is found. This may produce wrong overload selection or different error messages for edge-case calls with wrong arity.

ImporterResult.sourceMapUrl fallback data URI (line 43) uses 'data:application/octet-stream;utf-8,' with percent-encoding. Dart-sass result.dart:27 uses Uri.dataFromString(contents, encoding: utf8) which produces 'data:text/plain;charset=utf-8,...' potentially with base64 encoding. MIME type and encoding differ.

ISS-585 should be reopened: _runBuiltInCallable exists (line 2426) but is annotated @nowarn(unused) and never called. visitFunctionExpression (line 1342) calls bic.callback(padded) directly, bypassing the structured dispatch with callbackFor overload resolution, rest-argument assembly, keyword checking, and _addExceptionSpan wrapping that _runBuiltInCallable implements. The function dispatch should route through _runBuiltInCallable for BuiltInCallable, not call callback directly.
_writeLine uses zeroed source instead of copying last entry source fields when _inSpan is true
withDeprecationWarning wraps only main callback, not per-overload callbacks
callbackFor missing fuzzy-match overload selection with minMismatchDistance
data URI uses application/octet-stream MIME type instead of text/plain;charset=utf-8
_runBuiltInCallable fully ported but unused. Inline dispatch in visitFunctionExpression is functionally equivalent but bypasses callbackFor. Structural gap, not correctness bug.

SerializeVisitor.recordMapping (line 131-132) calls buffer.forSpan(span)(()) with Unit callback, so _inSpan is true only during the empty callback, then immediately restored. Original dart-sass _for (serialize.dart:1675) wraps the ENTIRE content emission callback in forSpan, keeping _inSpan=true throughout. This affects SourceMapBuffer._writeLine which copies span info to new lines only when _inSpan=true. Multi-line nodes (comments, custom properties) will not propagate source spans across line breaks. Additionally, recordMapping is only called at 3 sites (style rule, declaration name, declaration value) while the original uses _for at 10 sites: visitCssComment:197, visitCssAtRule:223, visitCssMediaRule:242, visitCssImport:264+267, visitCssKeyframeBlock:299, visitCssStyleRule:335, visitCssSupportsRule:343, visitCssDeclaration:367, _write:1679.

SerializeVisitor missing 4 constructor parameters from original _SerializeVisitor (serialize.dart:153-171): useSpaces (default true, controls space vs tab indentation), indentWidth (default 2, configurable 0-10), lineFeed (default LineFeed.lf, could be cr/crlf/lf/lfcr), and logger (for warnings). writeIndent (line 171-178) hardcodes 2-space indentation instead of using _indentCharacter * _indentWidth. writeLine (line 180-181) hardcodes \n instead of using _lineFeed.text. The top-level serialize() entry point (line 141) and companion-object methods also lack these parameters. The original validates indentWidth with RangeError.checkValueInInterval(0, 10).

Environment.setVariable (lines 562-604) does not propagate nodeWithSpan to Module.setVariable in 3 delegation paths: (1) line 563: _getModule(namespace.get).setVariable(name, value) — missing nodeWithSpan, (2) line 582: moduleWithName.get.setVariable(name, value) — missing nodeWithSpan, (3) line 604: m.setVariable(name, value) — missing nodeWithSpan. Original dart-sass environment.dart:579,601,618 always passes nodeWithSpan through all three delegation paths. This causes variableNodes maps to not be updated when writing through module boundaries, losing source span tracking for variable assignments.

NoSourceMapBuffer missing clear() and buildSourceMap() methods that throw UnsupportedOperationException. Original no_source_map_buffer.dart:28-33 has both methods throwing. Port's NoSourceMapBuffer (lines 40-60) lacks both. Since SassBuffer trait does not declare these methods and they are never called polymorphically on NoSourceMapBuffer, this has no runtime impact, but deviates from the original's API surface.

Module.setVariable parameter type changed from AstNode to Nullable[FileSpan]. Original module.dart:75 takes 'AstNode nodeWithSpan' (non-nullable, required). Port Module.scala:95 takes 'nodeWithSpan: Nullable[FileSpan] = Nullable.Null' (nullable, optional). Two differences: (1) type is FileSpan not AstNode — callers must extract span before calling, losing the lazy-span benefit documented in module.dart:28-31, (2) parameter is optional with null default — callers can omit it, losing span tracking. All 4 implementations (BuiltInModule:152, ForwardedView:228, ShadowedView:388, EnvironmentModule:1261) consistently use the new signature.

SourceMapBuffer.forSpan (lines 106-113) does not use try/finally to restore _inSpan. Original source_map_buffer.dart:42-56 wraps the callback in try { return callback(); } finally { _inSpan = wasInSpan; }, ensuring _inSpan is restored even if the callback throws. The port directly assigns result = callback without exception safety, so _inSpan would remain true if the callback threw. In practice, serialization exceptions are fatal, but this deviates from the original's defensive pattern.

FINDING-1: module-functions, module-mixins, get-function, and get-mixin create SassFunction/SassMixin instances without withCompileContext binding. Original evaluate.dart lines 470-471, 485-486, 501-502, 517, 537 use SassFunction.withCompileContext(value, _compileContext) and SassMixin.withCompileContext(value, _compileContext). The port's SassFunction/SassMixin classes have the assertCompileContext/withCompileContext machinery but it is never invoked. A SassFunction from one compilation can be used in another without error. Low practical impact in single-threaded usage.

FINDING-2: meta.call() (lines 495-541) does not use the proper argument binding pipeline. Original evaluate.dart lines 540-599 constructs a synthetic ArgumentList AST node with rest/keywordRest expressions and calls _runFunctionCallable which goes through the full evaluator binding pipeline (ParameterList.verify, default values, rest arg assembly, keyword rest handling). The port directly slices args.tail and dispatches through CurrentCallableInvoker, bypassing: (a) synthetic ArgumentList construction, (b) _runFunctionCallable binding, (c) keywordRest argument handling. Complex call() invocations with default parameters or keyword rest will behave differently.

ImportCache missing _parseSelectors field from original import_cache.dart:43. The original passes parseSelectors to Stylesheet.parse (line 343) which controls whether StyleRule.parsedSelector is eagerly populated. The port's ImportCache constructors (lines 47-60) don't accept or store this flag, and importCanonical (line 266) doesn't pass it to the parser. All three original constructors (primary:102, none:107, only:113) accept this parameter.

FINDING-5: nestFn (lines 162-193) parses ALL selectors with allowParent=true, including the first. Original selector.dart:55 uses allowParent: !first, meaning the first selector is parsed with allowParent=false, disallowing bare & (parent selectors). The port then adds an after-the-fact check (lines 174-186) that only rejects parent selectors WITH SUFFIXES (e.g. &-foo) but accepts bare &. This means selector-nest(&, .bar) passes in the port but should fail per dart-sass. Fix: parse first selector with allowParent=false like the original.

FINDING-6+7: visitLegacyIfExpression (lines 1454-1468) does not call _evaluateMacroArguments (original evaluate.dart:3862-3926, 65 LOC) or _verifyArguments (evaluate.dart:3961-3970). _evaluateMacroArguments handles rest-arg expansion: when if() is called with a rest argument (...), the original evaluates the rest expression and expands it into positional/named. The port directly accesses node.arguments.positional/named (the raw AST), which works for the common 3-arg case but fails when rest/splat args are used. _verifyArguments validates arity against LegacyIfExpression.declaration ParameterList.

FINDING-9: _resolvePackageExports extension check (lines 183-187) is too restrictive. Original node_package.dart:181 checks p.url.extension(subpath).isNotEmpty (ANY extension present), returning null to skip index-variant fallback. Port only checks if extension is in ValidExtensions (.scss/.sass/.css). A subpath like 'utils.js' would have the original return null immediately but the port would fall through to index-variant resolution. Fix: change condition to check for any non-empty extension, not just Sass extensions.

FINDING-10: canonicalize (line 54) always uses _entryPointDirectory for pkg: URL base directory resolution. Original node_package.dart:46-48 uses containingUrl when its scheme is 'file' (containingUrl?.scheme == 'file' ? p.dirname(p.fromUri(containingUrl!)) : _entryPointDirectory). This means pkg: URLs inside nested node_modules packages will not resolve relative to the importing file's directory. Fix: accept containingUrl and use it when available.

FINDING-11: canonicalize does not check for URL authority (host/port/user). Original node_package.dart:36-37 throws 'A pkg: URL must not have a host, port, username or password.' when url.hasAuthority is true. Port skips this validation entirely.

ISS-929 NOT FIXED: commit 1ea2aa0e claims to have added _for(node)(callback) wrapping, _writeCssValue helper, and removed recordMapping, but SerializeVisitor.scala was NOT modified in that commit. The file still has (1) degenerate recordMapping(span) at line 131 calling forSpan with Unit callback, (2) no _for method, (3) no _writeCssValue method, (4) only 3 of 10 dart-sass _for() call sites covered. All 10 call sites need proper forSpan wrapping: visitCssComment(line 1921), visitCssAtRule(1949), visitCssMediaRule(1966), visitCssImport(2033), visitCssKeyframeBlock(2070), visitCssStyleRule(1440), visitCssSupportsRule(2018), visitCssDeclaration name(1710), visitCssDeclaration value(1733), plus _write(CssValue) helper missing entirely.

visitCssStyleRule line 1441 uses recordMapping(node.span) but dart-sass uses _for(node.selector, ...) at serialize.dart:335 — the span should be node.selector's span, not node's span. visitCssDeclaration line 1733 uses recordMapping(node.span) for the value emission but dart-sass uses _buffer.forSpan(node.valueSpanForMap, ...) at serialize.dart:377 — the span should be node.valueSpanForMap, not node.span. The declaration name at line 1710 uses recordMapping(node.span) but dart-sass uses _write(node.name) which calls _for(node.name, ...) — the span should be node.name's span.

protocolRegex missing case-insensitive flag: original JS regex has /i flag on pattern /^[\x00-\x20]*([^\\/#?]*?)(:|&#0*58|&#x0*3a|&colon)/i but Scala port at line 93 omits (?i). This means HTML entity variants like &#X0*3A or &COLON won't match. Add (?i) prefix to the regex pattern.

Style companion object exposes 4 extra cramped variants (DISPLAY_CRAMPED, TEXT_CRAMPED, SCRIPT_CRAMPED, SCRIPTSCRIPT_CRAMPED at lines 120-123) that are NOT in the original TypeScript export (Style.ts lines 124-129 only exports DISPLAY, TEXT, SCRIPT, SCRIPTSCRIPT). While these may be useful for internal use, they expand the public API beyond what the original provides. If intentional for Scala convenience, add a comment documenting this as a deliberate addition.

VERB_REGEX_BROKEN: [^] on line 93 is invalid Java regex syntax (PatternSyntaxException at runtime). In JavaScript [^] means 'any character including newline' but java.util.regex.Pattern rejects it as 'Unclosed character class'. Must replace ([^]) with ([\s\S]) or (.) with Pattern.DOTALL on the entire pattern. Both \\verb\\*([^])[\\s\\S]*?\\4 (line 93) and \\verb([^*a-zA-Z])[\\s\\S]*?\\5 (line 94) are affected — the second line's [^*a-zA-Z] is valid Java regex, only the first line's [^] is broken.

VERB_DOTALL_SEMANTICS: Lines 93-94 use [\s\S]*? to replace JS .*? (non-greedy dot). In the original JS, .*? does NOT match newline characters (no /s flag), so \verb content stops at the first newline even if the delimiter hasn't appeared. The Scala port's [\s\S]*? matches across newlines, subtly broadening the match. While the lazy quantifier + backreference usually makes this harmless, for strict fidelity the port should use a negation class excluding newline: [^\n]*? or use Pattern.compile without DOTALL and use .*? directly.

COMMENT_UNICODE_RANGE_DROPPED: Line 56 comment says 'does not match the BMP private use area -' but the original (Lexer.ts line 28) says 'does not match the BMP private use area \uE000-\uF8FF'. The Unicode range was dropped from the comment.

SIZING_SIZE_TYPE: ParseNodeSizing.size is typed as Double (line 669) but the original (parseNode.ts line 436) uses 'size: number' which in context is always an integer (1-10 sizing levels). Should be Int for fidelity with the original semantics, matching how ParseNodeDelimsizing.size is already Int (line 393).

DEFINE_FUNCTION_BUILDERS_MATHML_OPTIONAL: defineFunctionBuilders at line 226 makes mathmlBuilder optional (Nullable.Null default) but the original (defineFunction.ts line 196-201) requires mathmlBuilder as a mandatory parameter. While this doesn't cause runtime errors since the internal defineFunction handles Nullable, it weakens the API contract.

Heap index arithmetic off-by-one: heapify uses 2*i instead of 2*i+1 for left child (line 122), decrease uses idx>>1 instead of (idx-1)>>1 for parent (line 147). Both produce incorrect heap operations for 0-based arrays, breaking priority queue behavior.

Missing 7 of 27 layout pipeline phases: injectEdgeLabelProxies, normalizeRanks, assignRankMinMax, removeEdgeLabelProxies, normalize.run call, parentDummyChains module, addBorderSegments module, removeBorderNodes. Also Nesting.cleanup is at phase 18 instead of phase 8. Without these, edges spanning multiple ranks have no dummy nodes, edge labels are not positioned via ranking, compound graph borders are not handled.

feasibleTree creates duplicate edge then overwrites (lines 112-114). calcCutValue and exchangeEdges overload minRank/maxRank fields for low/lim tree numbering, destroying rank data. exchangeEdges uses incorrect rank shifting instead of original updateRanks preorder traversal. Need dedicated low/lim fields and faithful updateRanks port.

Missing 5 of 8 original order module files: barycenter.js, sort-subgraph.js, resolve-conflicts.js, sort.js, build-layer-graph.js, add-subgraph-constraints.js (total ~425 LOC). Port uses simplified direct barycenter sort that cannot handle compound graph subgraph constraints. LOC ratio 0.35.

undo does not restore original edges via g.setEdge(edgeObj, origLabel). Missing dummyChains field in GraphLabel. run does not record chain heads. Walk-based point collection may double-process shared dummy nodes.

Missing treeDepths (nesting-graph.js:100-112), sumWeights (nesting-graph.js:117-125), and proper nesting edge weight formula (height + sumWeights + 1). Uses hardcoded weight=0 which causes ordering to incorrectly prioritize nesting edges.
Uses return statement at line 135 (buildLayerMatrix). Missing successorWeights, predecessorWeights, normalizeRanks functions from original dagre/util.js.

[R0610-P1] pureGetters is typed String (CompressorOptions.scala:183) but terser pure_getters is boolean|"strict" (index.js:258). Since option("pure_getters") returns the String, the `== true` boolean-gated paths — destructuring prune (Compressor.scala:4562, index.js:4077) and drop-unused keep (DropUnused.scala:848, drop-unused.js:331) — can NEVER fire because a user cannot set pureGetters to boolean true; they are dead under every config. Default "strict" behavior is unaffected (faithful, see ISS-1037 green guard). Done = model pure_getters as boolean|"strict" (union/ADT) so option("pure_getters")==true is reachable, + a differential test proving destructuring prune fires under pure_getters=true matching terser. Discovered by ISS-1037 reproducer.
